  "sound_source": "water hammer",

  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Severe water hammer (hydraulic shock) occurs immediately after the fixture valve closes.",
    "Lack of adequate pipe securing or missing water hammer arrestors on quick-closing valves."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install water hammer arrestors conforming to ASSE 1010 at the quick-closing valves.",
    "Inspect and secure loose PEX and copper piping with approved hangers and straps to prevent movement."
  ],
  "rationale": "The audio recording clearly demonstrates a sharp, metallic banging sound (water hammer) immediately following the rapid closure of a fixture valve. This hydraulic shock can damage joints, fittings, and appliances over time, violating standard plumbing installation codes that require control of water hammer.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 604.9 (Water hammer)",
    "UPC Section 609.10 (Water Hammer Arrestors)"
  ]
}
